candidates.title: Read Rights or It's Out | Miranda v. Arizona
candidates.style: Codex-generated interrogation room, handcuffed hands, red/blue police light, baked-in large title
candidates.reason: Stronger than v002: clearer mobile read, more cinematic, more immediate arrest/interrogation signal, and still no identifiable face.

candidates.title: Read Rights or It's Out | Miranda v. Arizona
candidates.style: red alert, handcuffs, large two-line text
candidates.reason: Highest mobile contrast, immediate arrest cue, no real-person likeness, strongest conflict hook.

candidates.title: He Won, Still Guilty | Miranda v. Arizona
candidates.reason: Strong twist but less instantly legible than the red handcuffs concept.

candidates.title: Police Must Say This | Miranda v. Arizona
candidates.reason: Clear and accurate enough for discovery, but less urgent than red A.

candidates.title: The 4 Sentences That Rewrote Every U.S. Arrest
candidates.reason: Accurate, but too text-heavy and less flashy.
